name: plate-handler
description: Implement rich text editors using Plate.js. Supports creating both simple (comment/chat) and detailed (document/blog) editors.
capabilities: Install Plate.js components via shadcn CLI commands
Create simple editors for comments, chat, and short text with basic formatting
Build detailed editors with headings, blockquotes, and lists
Configure toolbars with formatting buttons and mark controls
Implement state management for editor content using value and onChange props
Follow best practices for plugin registration and component architecture
author: AayushBaniya2006
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing implementation guides and code examples for Plate.js editors. All 70 static findings are false positives - they detected bash commands, code patterns, and URLs within markdown documentation and JSON metadata files, not executable code. No actual security risks identified.
valueStatement: Building rich text editors from scratch is complex and time-consuming. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for implementing Plate.js editors, from simple comment inputs to full-featured document editors with headings, lists, and media support.
userTitle: Add Plate.js rich text editors to React apps
limitations: Provides implementation guidance only, not pre-built components
Requires existing shadcn/ui setup in the target project
Does not cover server-side rendering or backend storage implementation
Assumes basic familiarity with React and TypeScript

useCases.title: Add comment systems with rich text
useCases.description: Implement rich text commenting in React applications with bold, italic, and underline support for user feedback

useCases.title: Build blog and document editors
useCases.description: Create full-featured document editors for CMS with headings, lists, blockquotes, and media support

useCases.title: Standardize editor components
useCases.description: Establish consistent editor patterns across multiple projects using Plate.js and shadcn/ui design systems

promptTemplates.title: Install core components
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I install the core Plate.js editor and toolbar components using the shadcn CLI in my React project?

promptTemplates.title: Create simple comment editor
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a simple Plate.js editor for comments with only bold, italic, and underline formatting in a React component

promptTemplates.title: Build detailed blog editor
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a detailed Plate.js editor with H1, H2, H3 headings, blockquotes, lists, and a full toolbar for blog post creation

promptTemplates.title: Customize toolbar and plugins
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I add custom toolbar buttons, configure plugin components, and implement state management in a detailed Plate.js editor?

bestPractices: Use withComponent() when registering plugins that render custom elements like headings and blockquotes
Store editor content as JSON using onChange callback rather than HTML for better security and flexibility
Install base UI components via shadcn CLI to ensure consistency with your existing design system
antiPatterns: Avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML for editable content; use Plate value prop and JSON serialization instead
Do not skip component registration for plugins that render custom elements or they will not display correctly
Avoid hardcoding toolbar layouts; use the FixedToolbar and FloatingToolbar components for proper structure

faq.question: Does Plate.js work with Next.js applications?
faq.answer: Yes, use 'use client' directive since Plate.js requires client-side rendering. All editor components must be client components in Next.js.

faq.question: What is the difference between simple and detailed editors?
faq.answer: Simple editors have basic marks like bold and italic. Detailed editors include block elements like headings, lists, quotes, tables, and media.

faq.question: How do I save editor content to a database?
faq.answer: Use the onChange prop to capture the value as JSON. Serialize to your preferred format (JSON, HTML, or markdown) before storing in your database.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: This skill only provides implementation guidance and documentation. It does not access your files, make network requests, or execute code.

faq.question: Why is my toolbar not appearing in the editor?
faq.answer: Ensure FixedToolbar is placed inside the Plate provider component and that toolbar button components are properly imported from shadcn/ui directory.

faq.question: How does Plate.js compare to other editors like Slate or TipTap?
faq.answer: Plate.js is built on Slate architecture with a plugin-based approach. It integrates well with shadcn/ui and provides excellent TypeScript support.
